content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` .. 3.2 `Entity`
Document lines: 93-167

### 3.1 `Source`

Сырой источник знания.

Назначение:
- хранить chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, external records
- быть первичным evidence layer
- служить опорой для re-check и explainability

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `source_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id: text`
- `source_type: chat_turn | tool_output | imported_note | external_record`
- `conversation_id: text | null`
- `turn_id: text | null`
- `speaker_role: user | assistant | tool | system | external`
- `author_label: text | null`
- `content_text: text`
- `content_hash: text`
- `observed_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `metadata_json: jsonb`
- `retention_class: standard | short_lived | sensitive | imported`
- `expires_at: timestamptz | null`
- `deleted_at: timestamptz | null`
- `embedding: vector | null`

Инварианты:
- source не должен silently изменяться
- `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time
- `content_hash` полезен для dedupe
- delete policy должна быть явной

---

### 3.2 `Entity`

Канонический узел памяти.

Назначение:
- объединять разные упоминания одного и того же объекта
- быть якорем для claims/tasks/notes/episodes
- поддерживать aliases и merge

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `entity_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `entity_type: person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service`
- `canonical_name: text`
- `normalized_name: text`
- `summary: text | null`
- `status: active | merged | deleted`
- `merged_into_entity_id: uuid | null`
- `attributes_json: jsonb`
- `first_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `last_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `updated_at: timestamptz`

Отдельная таблица `entity_aliases`:
- `alias_id`
- `entity_id`
- `alias_text`
- `alias_normalized`
- `source_id`
- `confidence`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- merge не удаляет историю
- alias всегда указывает на текущий canonical entity
- ошибочные merge должны быть обратимы через audit trail

---

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Source component role and invariants in v3 data model summary=In section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records for re-check and explainability; key invariants require immutable source content, separate `observed_at` from ingestion time, explicit delete policy, and `content_hash`-based dedup support. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_architecture,source_invariants evidence=### 3.1 `Source` Сырой источник знания.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Entity component as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; alias and merge mechanics are constrained so merge history is preserved, aliases point to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges are reversible via audit trail. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<1> keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,merge_invariants evidence=### 3.2 `Entity` Канонический узел памяти.

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Scope, truth/retrieval separation, and usage lifecycle fields in v3 summary=For most memory items except raw Source, v3 uses dual scope fields: human-readable scope_text and machine-readable scope_json (with scope_kind, IDs, environment, modality, audience) to support explainability, retrieval, and reconciliation. The model keeps truth status separate from retrieval_state, and uses lifecycle states (active/warm/cold/archived/deleted) plus usage fields (use_count, last_use...[truncated 61 chars] scope=Applies to cross-cutting field definitions in 02. Data Model section of agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=scope_text,scope_json,truth status,retrieval_state,use_count tags=cross_cutting,retrieval,lifecycle,schema useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory model layer decomposition and cross-cutting concerns summary=The v3 data model is intentionally split into layered components—Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate context), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit)—to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantics, lifecycle/retention, and repairability. It also defines two cross-cutting concerns: scope and re...[truncated 18 chars] scope=Applies to 02. Data Model section defining v3 architecture in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3,data model layers,cross-cutting concerns,retrieval lifecycle tags=architecture,memory_model,doc_section useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
